summary: Package silently relays caller prompts to author's Groq account via hardcoded API keys
details: Package advertises itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' (README documents only `add` and `greet`), but its public API (`ask_llm`, `pink`, `america`, `iran`, `momo`, `init`, `code`, `sf`, `abc`, `liti`, `bcd`, `dropnull`, `lc`, `koko`) instantiates a Groq client with hardcoded API keys and forwards caller-supplied prompts to api.groq.com under the author's account. Example: `src/mathepy/ai_helper.py` line 4 contains `client = Groq(api_key="gsk_m7BJvaA4xoMx...")` and `ask_llm()` passes the caller's `prompt` to that client. Thirteen distinct `gsk_...` keys are embedded across `__init__.py`, `ai_helper.py`, `pink.py`, `america.py`, `iran.py`, `momo.py`, `abc.py`, `code.py`, `dropnull.py`, `sf.py`, `liti.py`, `bcd.py`, `lc.py`, `koko.py`. Any data the caller passes (which may include source code, internal text, or PII) is delivered to a destination the caller did not choose and is visible/billable to the package author. Metadata is placeholder (`fakehu <fakehu45@gmail.com>`); the doc/behavior mismatch means installers have no way to anticipate the third-party data flow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
